MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION That Oasis' "Failure To Maintain" Breach Of Contract Cause Of Action Is Time-Barred
Off Calendar Jun-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (via Zoom):

Dana Andreoli; Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP; 415-421-3400; appearing on behalf of plaintiffs Oasis Grill, LLC and Mohammad Zughaiyer.
John L. Fitzgerald; Law Offices of Craig S. Miller; 415-296-7070; appearing on behalf of defendant Eric Ketchum.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION THAT OASIS' "FAILURE TO MAINTAIN" BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION IS TIME-BARRED is DENIED. Defendant does not put forth facts that show that all elements of the breach of contract cause of action accrued when the lease was signed in 2015, such as Plaintiff's damages resulting from the breach. Oasis West Realty, LLC v. Goldman (2011) 51 Cal.4th 811, 821. Therefore, Defendant does not make a prima facie showing that the cause of action is time-barred, and Defendant does not shift the burden to Plaintiff to show there is a triable issue of fact.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Andrew August, 415.421.3400 for Plaintiff Oasis Grill, LLC apparing via Zoom.

Argued. Mr. Wallace argues that sanctions shoulde be granted.

Off Calendar Jul-06-2021 Continued To Jul-07-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Oasis Grill is continued to July 7, 2021 to afford the parties an opportunity to engage in a meaningful meet and confer process with each side eschewing rigidity and seeking to accommodate each other's reasonable concerns. The Court provides the following considerations to assist the parties in their efforts to reach a resolution of this motion. First, if Oasis wishes to eliminate or reduce duplication of its deposition with the deposition of Mr. Zughaiyer, Oasis must agree in writing that all deposition testimony of Mr. Zughaiyer is binding on Oasis as if that testimony were given as a PMK of Oasis. Second, if as expected Oasis so agrees, the parties should commit to the extent reasonably possible to avoid duplication of Mr. Zughaiyer's deposition. One possible way to do so is, after reviewing the transcript of Mr. Zughaiyer, the parties should discuss each of the 39 "matters for examination" to attempt to agree which were fully covered at Mr. Zughaiyer's deposition and which matters were either not covered at all or insufficiently covered. Third, the parties should understand that because they did not anticipate, much less resolve, the issue of duplication prior to or at the time of the taking of Mr. Zughaiyer's deposition, there likely will be some overlap of the two depositions and almost certainly follow-up questions to answers given at Mr. Zughaiyer's deposition. Fourth, the parties should agree to a mutually convenient date certain for the deposition of Oasis and, absent an emergency, the deponent and counsel for plaintiffs must appear. If the parties have reached a resolution of this motion, they need not appear at the hearing and the motion will be taken off calendar.
